# Strix Field Guide

> A concept-level field guide to the Strix codebase, written by Doc Holiday
> (https://doc.holiday) by exploring the Strix source directly. Strix is an open-source
> autonomous AI penetration-testing tool built on the OpenAI Agents SDK. Each page explains
> why a subsystem exists, its central abstractions, and how it relates to its neighbors.
> Every doc page is also served as plain markdown at <page-url>/index.md.

## Pages

- [The Big Picture](/docs/the-big-picture/): the map — Strix as a pentest app built on the OpenAI Agents SDK, and the shape of one scan
- [Anatomy of a Scan](/docs/anatomy-of-a-scan/): one `strix` run traced end to end, including how the agent is prompted to behave like a pentester
- [The Graph of Agents](/docs/the-graph-of-agents/): how the AgentCoordinator grows a tree of agents and how they coordinate through shared sessions
- [The Agent Loop](/docs/the-agent-loop/): what "think-plan-act-observe" really is — the SDK Runner loop plus Strix's lifecycle-gating, budget, and parking
- [The Docker Sandbox](/docs/the-docker-sandbox/): the per-scan Docker container — the isolation boundary, its lifecycle, and the SDK-subclass customizations
- [The Toolkit Layer](/docs/the-toolkit-layer/): the two tool families — host-side function tools vs in-container SDK capabilities — and how the model reaches them
- [Traffic Interception](/docs/traffic-interception/): why all container traffic flows through the Caido proxy, and how the browser rides on it
- [Telemetry Logging and Usage](/docs/telemetry-logging-and-usage/): what Strix records about a run — analytics, per-scan logs, and the LLM usage/cost ledger
- [From Finding to Report](/docs/from-finding-to-report/): the vulnerability report model — how findings are captured, deduped, and written out as artifacts
- [About This Site](/docs/about-this-site/): scope, audience, provenance

## See also

- Official Strix documentation: https://docs.strix.ai
- Strix source: https://github.com/usestrix/strix
